title: Osmolindsaea odorata
taxonomy: Osmolindsaea odorata is a genus of flowering plants in the family Lindsaeaceae, which is a group of ferns. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The classification of Osmolindsaea odorata is further divided into the class Polypodiopsida, which is a subclass of the larger group Polypodiidae. This fern is native to the tropical regions of the Old World, specifically in Asia and the Pacific. Osmolindsaea odorata is a relatively rare and understudied genus, adding to its unique and fascinating classification within the plant kingdom.
